name: Sci-Hub MCP Server
what_it_does: Sci-Hub MCP server enabling AI agents to retrieve academic papers via Sci-Hub — fetching full-text PDFs of research papers by DOI, PMID, or URL, bypassing academic publisher paywalls. Primarily used by researchers in institutions without journal subscriptions for accessing scientific literature.
use_cases: Retrieving research papers by DOI from academic research agents
Accessing full-text scientific literature for analysis from literature review agents
Obtaining papers not available through institutional subscriptions from research agents
Building academic knowledge bases from scientific papers from research pipeline agents
not_for: Commercial or enterprise research (copyright law applies — use legal alternatives like PubMed, arXiv, or library access)
Jurisdictions where Sci-Hub access is blocked or explicitly illegal (many countries)
Academic institutions bound by publisher agreements that prohibit Sci-Hub use
best_when: Individual researchers in regions with limited academic library access need to retrieve specific papers by DOI for personal research — most users are in developing countries or institutions without journal subscriptions.
avoid_when: You have institutional journal access, operate commercially, are bound by publisher agreements, or are in a jurisdiction where Sci-Hub is legally blocked. Always prefer legal alternatives: PubMed Central (free), arXiv (preprints), Unpaywall (legal open access), or direct author contact.

auth.notes: No authentication — accesses publicly accessible Sci-Hub web interface. Sci-Hub URL may change (mirror sites). No API key required.

pricing.notes: Sci-Hub is free. MCP server is free. Legal status varies significantly by jurisdiction — review applicable copyright law.

performance.rate_limits: Sci-Hub may throttle or block high-frequency requests

known_agent_gotchas: LEGAL: Sci-Hub bypasses copyright — use is legally ambiguous in most jurisdictions and explicitly illegal in some (US, EU); consult legal counsel before enterprise use
Sci-Hub mirrors change frequently — hardcoded URLs will break; URL may need regular updates
Paper availability is not guaranteed — some papers are not in Sci-Hub's database
PDF quality varies — some papers are scans rather than digital originals
Unreliable availability — Sci-Hub faces ongoing legal pressure and intermittent downtime
Prefer legal alternatives: arXiv (preprints), PubMed Central (NIH papers), Unpaywall (legal open access), or direct author email
agent_readiness.security_notes: Accesses third-party site (Sci-Hub) over HTTPS. No credentials. Copyright violation risk — evaluate legal status in your jurisdiction. For legitimate academic access, prefer PubMed Central, arXiv, Unpaywall, or institutional library access.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. No credentials. Copyright law compliance is the primary concern — not a traditional security issue. Legal risk is significant in many jurisdictions.
